title: Unique Umami Slovakian Rhubarb Dumplings
description: A sour and bouncy Slovakian dish featuring rhubarb and dashi extract, perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the rhubarb, dashi extract, and salt, and let it sit for 10 minutes to allow the rhubarb to release its juice
In a separate bowl, whisk together the potato starch, eggs, and black pepper to form a dough
Add the vegetable oil to the dough and mix until it is well combined
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of the rhubarb mixture in the center of each disk
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling
Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the dumplings for 10 minutes, or until they float to the surface
Remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and top with chopped green onions
